Only the 20 fastest candidates have their individual data printed in the paper's tables (basic parameters, kinematics, escape velocities, unbound probabilities, atmospheric parameters). This extraction identifies those 20 fastest candidates as possibly unbound from the Galaxy, based on the paper's unbound probability assessment under seven Galactic potential models. The remaining 571 candidates are not individually accessible in the provided paper assets (external CSV).","tooling":null},"method_chain":[{"id":"step-01","depends_on":[],"step_type":"input_catalog","summary":"LAMOST DR7 low-resolution spectroscopic survey provides radial velocities and atmospheric parameters; Gaia DR2 provides five-parameter astrometry (positions, proper motions, parallax) and photometry.","inputs":[],"outputs":["LAMOST DR7 catalog","Gaia DR2 catalog"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":188,"end_line":194,"context":"description of LAMOST DR7 and photometry"}]},{"id":"step-02","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"cross_match","summary":"Cross-match LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 using TOPCAT within a 5 arcsec radius, producing the LAMOST-Gaia catalog of over 10 million entries.","inputs":["LAMOST DR7 catalog","Gaia DR2 catalog"],"outputs":["LAMOST-Gaia radius"}]},{"id":"step-07","depends_on":[],"step_type":"solar_position_and_motion","summary":"Adopt solar position and motion parameters: distance to Galactic center R0 = 8.2 kpc, offset above disk z0 = 25 pc; circular velocity of LSR 238 km/s; solar peculiar motion [U, V, W] = [14.0, 12.24, 7.25] km/s.","inputs":[],"outputs":["solar parameters"],"parameters":[{"name":"R0","raw_value":"8.2 kpc","value":"8.2","unit":"kpc","description":"assumed distance from Sun to Galactic center","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":210,"end_line":214,"context":"solar position and motion values"}]},{"id":"step-05","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"radial_velocity_measurement","summary":"Use radial velocities from the LAMOST Stellar Parameter Pipeline (LASP). Apply a zero-point correction of -5.4 km/s derived from cross-match with a catalog of radial velocity standard stars.","inputs":["LAMOST DR7 spectra"],"outputs":["zero-point corrected LASP radial velocities"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":280,"end_line":288,"context":"LASP RV adopted and zero-point correction"}]},{"id":"step-06","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"distance_estimation","summary":"Heliocentric distance determined by inverting the parallax: d = 1/varpi. Only stars with positive parallax and fractional parallax error f_varpi <= 0.2 are used.","inputs":["Gaia DR2 parallax"],"outputs":["heliocentric distance"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":254,"end_line":259,"context":"distance determination by 1/parallax and low-f sample criteria"}]},{"id":"step-08","depends_on":["step-01","step-06","step-05","step-07"],"step_type":"velocity_calculation","summary":"Compute Galactocentric Cartesian coordinates (X,Y,Z) and velocities (Vx, Vy, Vz) by Monte Carlo sampling of the five Gaia astrometric parameters and the corrected LASP radial velocity. Use 1000 realizations; record median, 16th, and 84th percentiles. Total Galactocentric velocity V_GC is the magnitude of the velocity vector.","inputs":["Gaia astrometry","corrected RV","solar parameters"],"outputs":["Galactocentric position (x,y,z)","Galactocentric velocity (Vx,Vy,Vz,V_GC)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":235,"end_line":242,"context":"MC method for Galactocentric distance and velocity with uncertainties"}]},{"id":"step-09","depends_on":[],"step_type":"galactic_potential_model","summary":"Seven Galactic potential models are used to compute escape velocities: Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014, Xue+2008. The escape velocity is evaluated at each star's Galactocentric distance.","inputs":[],"outputs":["escape velocity tables for each model"],"parameters":[{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Watkins+2019","value":"Watkins+2019","unit":"","description":"Galactic potential model","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":275,"end_line":276,"context":"list of potential models"}]},{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Gnedin+2005","value":"Gnedin+2005","unit":"","description":"Galactic potential model","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":275,"end_line":276,"context":"list of potential models"}]},{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Kenyon+2014","value":"Kenyon+2014","unit":"","description":"Galactic potential model","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":275,"end_line":276,"context":"list of potential models"}]},{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Koposov+2010","value":"Koposov+2010","unit":"","description":"Galactic potential model","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":275,"end_line":276,"context":"list of potential models"}]},{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Paczynski+1990","value":"Paczynski+1990","unit":"","description":"Galactic potential model","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":275,"end_line":276,"context":"list of potential models"}]},{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"MWPotential2014","value":"MWPotential2014","unit":"","description":"Galactic potential model","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":275,"end_line":276,"context":"list of potential models"}]},{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"Xue+2008","value":"Xue+2008","unit":"","description":"Galactic potential model","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":275,"end_line":276,"context":"list of potential models"}]},{"name":"escape_velocity_definition","raw_value":"position-dependent escape velocity","value":"position-dependent escape velocity","unit":"","description":"The escape velocity is calculated at each star's Galactocentric distance using the respective potential model.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":478,"end_line":485,"context":"escape velocities computed for the 20 fastest"}]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":275,"end_line":276,"context":"seven Galactic potential models adopted"}]},{"id":"step-10","depends_on":["step-08","step-09"],"step_type":"escape_or_bound_assessment","summary":"For each star and each potential model, compute the unbound probability P_ub as the fraction of 1000 MC realizations where the Galactocentric total velocity V_GC exceeds the position-dependent escape velocity. Stars with P_ub > 50% are considered unbound.","inputs":["Galactocentric velocities and distances","escape velocities from each model"],"outputs":["unbound probability P_ub for each model"],"parameters":[{"name":"escape_velocity_definition","raw_value":"fraction of MC realizations with V_GC > V_esc","value":"fraction of MC realizations with V_GC > V_esc","unit":"","description":"definition of P_ub","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":481,"end_line":482,"context":"definition of unbound probability P_ub"}]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":481,"end_line":485,"context":"unbound probability definition and summary table"}]},{"id":"step-03","depends_on":["step-02","step-08","step-10"],"step_type":"sample_selection","summary":"From the LAMOST-Gaia catalog, select the 'low-f' sample (positive parallax, f_varpi <= 0.2). For these stars, compute V_GC and r_GC, then keep those with either V_GC escaping in at least one potential model or V_GC >= 450 km/s. Perform spectral quality check (keep highest S/N for multiple observations) and visual RV verification, finally obtaining 591 HiVelSCs.","inputs":["LAMOST-Gaia catalog","velocities","escape conditions"],"outputs":["591 HiVelSCs"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":253,"end_line":278,"context":"sample selection criteria and final HiVelSC sample"}]},{"id":"step-04","depends_on":["step-01","step-03"],"step_type":"astrometric_calibration","summary":"Apply conservative quality criteria from Lindegren+2018 and Marchetti+2019 to flag HiVelSCs with reliable Gaia astrometry (astrometric_flag = 1). 476 of the 591 HiVelSCs satisfy these criteria.","inputs":["Gaia DR2 astrometry","HiVelSCs"],"outputs":["astrometric_flag"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":516,"end_line":521,"context":"astrometric quality flag criteria"}]},{"id":"step-11","depends_on":["step-08","step-09"],"step_type":"orbit_integration","summary":"Perform Monte Carlo orbit integration using Galpy and the MWPotential2014 model. For each star, 1000 MC realizations are traced back up to 10 Gyr. Compute eccentricity e, maximum vertical distance Zmax, minimum disk-crossing radius r_min, and the energy difference E-Phi(infinity). Values provided only for bound stars with Zmax <= 200 kpc.","inputs":["Galactocentric phase-space coordinates","MWPotential2014"],"outputs":["e, Zmax, r_min, E-Phi(infinity)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":653,"end_line":667,"context":"orbit integration method and output parameters"}]},{"id":"step-12","depends_on":["step-11","step-10"],"step_type":"origin_assessment","summary":"Classify HiVelSCs into four origin types (HVS, HRS, RS, OUT) based on: Pgc (fraction of orbits with r_min < 1 kpc), PMW (fraction with r_min < 25 kpc), and unbound probability Pub (from step-10). Criteria defined in Table 4.","inputs":["r_min","P_ub","classification criteria"],"outputs":["Origin_Class (HVS, HRS, RS, OUT)"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex","start_line":702,"end_line":705,"context":"origin classification using Pgc, PMW, Pub"}]}],"candidates":[{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-001","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel1","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel1","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":43,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel1"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-002","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel2","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel2","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":44,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel2"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-003","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel3","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel3","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":45,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel3"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-004","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel4","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel4","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":46,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel4"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-005","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel5","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel5","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":47,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel5"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-006","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel6","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel6","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206/catalog_tables/table-001.ecsv","line":48,"column":"col_001","column_header":"ID","raw_value":"Hivel6"}]}]}},{"identifiers":{"record_id":"2011.10206:cand-007","paper_candidate_id":"Hivel7","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"Hivel7","source_refs":[{"kind":"ecsv_cell","path":"literature/2011.10206 message.reasoning_content:
We need to fix the validation errors: method_chain[2] (step-03) has depends_on ['step-02','step-08','step-10'], but step-08 and step-10 are not yet defined at step-03. Step-03 is sample selection, which logically depends on input catalogs (step-02 for cross-match) and probably the velocity calculation (step-08) and escape assessment (step-10) to apply the selection criteria? Actually, the text says: after computing velocities and escape conditions, they selected those with either escaping under at least one potential or V_GC >= 450 km/s. So the selection step depends on velocity calculation and escape assessment. But step-08 and step-10 are defined later in the current chain. The rule says "never renumber, reuse, or delete existing step ids — candidate records already reference them." But there are no candidate records yet that refer to step ids? The scaffold only had identifiers. However, we need to ensure the method_chain is topologically sorted: each step should depend only on steps defined earlier. So we must reorder the steps. The instruction says: "You may add or split method steps, but NEVER renumber, reuse, or delete existing step ids — candidate records already reference them." But we are in Stage 1, and no candidate records have been filled with method_refs yet; they only have identifiers. However, the instruction is to fix the scaffold, which includes the method_chain. Perhaps we should not renumber existing steps but we can adjust the order of steps in the JSON array without changing their ids? The error is that step-03 depends on step-08 and step-10, which are defined later. The solution is to move step-03 to after step-10 in the JSON array. The ids remain the same, just the order changes. The rule "never renumber, reuse, or delete existing step ids" might allow reordering as long as ids remain. Or we could modify depends_on to not include future steps, but the selection step indeed requires those later steps. If we keep the original order, we'd violate the dependency. So reordering is the proper fix. The instruction "You may add or split method steps" suggests we can add steps, but we can also reorder existing steps? I'll reorder to maintain dependency order: step-03 should appear after step-10. Also, step-04 depends on step-03, so must be after step-03. And step-08 etc. must be before step-03. So I'll reorder the array: start with step-01, step-02 (cross-match), step-07 (solar parameters), step-05 (RV measurement), step-06 (distance estimation), step-08 (velocity calculation), step-09 (potential models), step-10 (escape assessment), then step-03 (sample selection), step-04 (astrometric calibration), step-11 (orbit integration), step-12 (origin assessment). That should satisfy all depends_on references. I must ensure step-11 depends on step-08 and step-09, which it does. Step-12 depends on step-11 and step-10. All good.

Now I'll output the corrected JSON with the reordered method_chain, keeping the same ids but in a different order. Also, the extraction tooling is null, which is allowed for now. The candidate roster remains the same. The candidate_groups_considered remains. I'll minify the JSON.
